meeting.title: MeetMind Capstone — Sprint Review & Task Planning

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: Hey everyone, thanks for joining. Let's go through what we have for the MeetMind project and plan the next steps.

transcript.speaker: Aditya K
transcript.text: Sure. I've been working on the ML classifier pipeline. The Perceptron, KNN, and MLP models are all trained and the F1 scores follow the expected progression.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: That's great. So the MLP is the primary production model, right?

transcript.speaker: Aditya K
transcript.text: Yes, it uses a two-layer architecture with 256 and 128 neurons. We're getting around 85 percent accuracy on the test set.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: Let's discuss the NER module next. Where are we with the task extraction?

transcript.speaker: Aditya S
transcript.text: The BERT NER component is working. It detects person names for assignee resolution and picks up deadline keywords like Friday, Monday, next week.

transcript.speaker: Aditya S
transcript.text: But I noticed the confidence scores are a bit low when there's no explicit name mentioned. We should add better fallback rules.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: We have decided to keep a four-rule fallback system for assignee detection. First check NER entities, then self-assignment with I will patterns, then team assignment, and finally mark as unassigned.

transcript.speaker: Aditya VIT
transcript.text: On the summarisation side, the BART model is generating decent summaries. I tested it with a 2000-word transcript and it condensed it to about 150 words.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: Nice. What about the chunking for longer transcripts?

transcript.speaker: Aditya VIT
transcript.text: I implemented a two-pass system. First pass chunks the transcript into 1024-token segments, summarises each, then the second pass merges and summarises again.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: We've decided to use distilbart-cnn-12-6 instead of the full BART model because it's 40 percent faster on CPU with minimal quality loss.

transcript.speaker: Aditya K
transcript.text: Makes sense. For the demo, should I also add the model evaluation comparison chart to the Jupyter notebook?

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: Yes. Aditya K, please add the confusion matrix and F1 comparison plots to the experiments notebook by tomorrow.

transcript.speaker: Aditya S
transcript.text: I will update the NER extraction logic to handle edge cases like multiple names in a single sentence by Friday.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: Aditya VIT, can you write the project documentation covering the ML pipeline architecture? We need it for the final submission.

transcript.speaker: Aditya VIT
transcript.text: Sure, I will complete the documentation and include the system architecture diagram by next Monday.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: The presentation to our professor is scheduled for next Wednesday so everything needs to be ready before that.

transcript.speaker: Aditya K
transcript.text: Should we also prepare a live demo or just the slides?

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: We've decided to do both. We'll show the live terminal demo first and then walk through the architecture slides.

transcript.speaker: Aditya S
transcript.text: I think we also need to add proper error handling in the API endpoints before the demo.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: Good point. Aditya S, please review all the API error responses and add proper HTTP status codes by Thursday.

transcript.speaker: Aditya VIT
transcript.text: We should also test the email sending functionality before the presentation.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: I will configure the SMTP settings and run a test email to all team members by tonight.

transcript.speaker: Aditya Bharti
transcript.text: Alright, everyone knows their tasks. Let's sync again tomorrow evening to check progress. Good meeting everyone.
